display.purpose: Page-level titles only, one per screen.

title.purpose: Dialog titles, section and card headings.

label.purpose: Form labels, table column headers, badges.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Default card/panel shadow. Lifts a surface off the console plane, never implies importance.

shadows.name: field
shadows.purpose: Subtler shadow for inputs and outline buttons.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for every color/border/box-shadow state transition on the console.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition duration used across buttons, inputs, and nav items.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one action per screen that matters most: submit, create, save.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; background: oklch(0.42 0.16 264); color: oklch(1 0 0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.34 0.15 264); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; border: 1px solid oklch(0.42 0.16 264); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.52 0.19 264 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary actions (Cancel, Edit) placed next to a Primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.01 264); border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.22 0.02 264); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 oklch(0 0 0 / 0.03); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0.008 264); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.42 0.16 264); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.52 0.19 264 / 0.5); }

components.name: Card
components.description: The console's one container shape — a list row, a stat tile, a form wrapper.
components.css: .ds-card { background: oklch(1 0 0); color: oklch(0.22 0.02 264); border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.01 264); border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 oklch(0 0 0 / 0.05); padding: 24px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; margin: 0; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 264); margin: 0; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Text entry inside create/edit dialogs; height-matched to Primary/Outline buttons so forms and actions align on one baseline.
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 4px 12px; border: 1px solid oklch(0.91 0.01 264); border-radius: 8px; background: transparent; color: oklch(0.22 0.02 264); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 oklch(0 0 0 / 0.03); transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: oklch(0.55 0.02 264); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: oklch(0.42 0.16 264); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.52 0.19 264 / 0.5); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Signature component: the console's fixed rail. Mirrors fully in RTL (the rail flips sides, not just the text).
components.html: الجامعات
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; width: 100%; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; color: oklch(0.55 0.02 264); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0.008 264); color: oklch(0.22 0.02 264); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: oklch(0.94 0.02 264); color: oklch(0.42 0.16 264); } .ds-nav-icon { width: 18px; height: 18px; flex-shrink: 0; }

narrative.overview: Jam3ty Admin is the bridge, not the ship. A small crew runs the entire platform from here for hours at a time, every day — universities, people, courses, payments — so the room has to stay calm and legible under sustained use, never showy, never in the way. Every instrument reads clearly at a glance; nothing on the console exists to be admired, everything exists to be trusted and acted on immediately. The palette centers on a deep, deliberate indigo-blue — not the stock Tailwind blue-700 this system started from, but a richer, more particular shade that reads as confident rather than default. The rest of the surface stays quiet: near-white panels, a single warm-neutral ink, and one accent color used with discipline. This is not a dashboard dressed up to impress a first-time visitor; it explicitly rejects the generic 'SaaS admin template' look — dated card grids, timid gray-on-gray hierarchies, decoration for its own sake. Arabic is the primary reading direction here, French secondary; the console has to feel exactly as native and confident mirrored right-to-left as it does left-to-right — RTL is not a retrofit.
keyCharacteristics: One deliberate accent (Bridge Indigo), used with restraint against a near-white console
Flat surfaces with a whisper of ambient shadow, never a lifted, showy card
Confident, calm interaction: definite hover/focus states, no timidity, no flourish
Fully bidirectional: every layout, icon, and alignment decision is mirrored correctly in RTL

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Bridge Indigo (and its Deep/Blue/Tint siblings) is the only saturated color on the console outside of the destructive red. If a screen needs a second 'important' color, that's a sign the hierarchy is wrong, not that the palette needs a new entry.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Every weight and size on the console maps to Display/Title/Body/Label. No ad hoc text-lg font-semibold invented per-component.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Nothing on the console lifts on hover. State changes are communicated through color and border, never through a growing shadow.

dos: Do keep Bridge Indigo as the only saturated accent on any given screen; everything else is neutral or destructive red.
Do give every interactive element a definite hover and focus state — calm does not mean inert.
Do test every screen mirrored in RTL before shipping it; a layout that only works LTR is not done.
Do use the Ambient/Field shadow pair for all elevation — nothing on the console should ever look like it's floating above the page.
donts: Don't introduce a second saturated 'important' color without checking the One Accent Rule first.
Don't use border-left/border-right colored stripes as a status indicator on cards or list rows.
Don't add hover states that lift, scale, or grow a shadow — that's marketing-site motion vocabulary, not console vocabulary.
Don't ship a screen that shows an 'API not enabled yet' message or a dead button — every visible action must work, or must not be visible.
Don't let a dated 2014-admin-template feeling creep in: no dense unstyled tables, no gray-on-gray hierarchy, no decoration that doesn't aid comprehension.
